entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing natural resources to ensure sustainability and productivity in agriculture

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures, often with fragile agro-ecosystems

entities.name: LAND AND SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of land and soil quality leading to productivity decline

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY DECLINE
entities.description: The reduction in agricultural output per unit area

entities.name: HUNGER AND POVERTY
entities.description: Socio-economic conditions resulting from agricultural failure and resource depletion

entities.name: MODERN CROP VARIETIES
entities.description: Genetically improved crop types designed for higher productivity

entities.name: HIGH YIELDS
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to produce a large amount of agricultural output

entities.name: CROP MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Agricultural practices used to optimize the growth and yield of crops

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

entities.name: MARKET AND INVESTMENT ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The economic context including market opportunities and investment availability

entities.name: INPUT SUPPLY SYSTEMS
entities.description: The systems responsible for providing seeds, fertilizers, and other inputs to farmers

entities.name: POLICY
entities.description: Governmental regulations and frameworks affecting agricultural research and adoption

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Specific technical interventions developed to manage natural resources

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC AND INSTITUTIONAL ISSUES
entities.description: Social, economic, and organizational factors that influence the adoption of agricultural technologies

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL INNOVATIONS
entities.description: New technologies or methods introduced to improve agricultural productivity and sustainability

entities.name: RAINFED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems that rely solely on rainfall for water supply

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of altering the genetic makeup of crops to improve desirable traits

entities.name: INTEGRATED GENETIC AND NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A paradigm that integrates crop genetics and natural resource management for sustainable agriculture

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The patterns of crops grown on a piece of land and their management

entities.name: CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Weather patterns and climate variability affecting agricultural production

entities.name: SYSTEMS APPROACH
entities.description: A holistic method of analyzing the rural economy, including crops, livestock, and socio-economic factors

entities.name: TRADITIONAL FOOD GRAINS
entities.description: Staple grains traditionally grown for food security

entities.name: CASH CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown primarily for sale and profit

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Domesticated animals raised in farm systems

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country in Asia where IGNRM is implemented
